threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can always spot a newbie by how they stress about the bus schedules; veterans know it’s all about the trains and when to walk.
Last night on the 133, a guy serenaded his mates with Elvis all the way from Brixton to London Bridge. Never thought I'd hear 'Can't Help Falling in Love' over the engine noise.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a saxophonist outside Rewe playing a jazz version of 'Schwarze Augen' while an elderly man danced with his shopping bags. That’s Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever noticed the spontaneous techno parties in the most random backyards? Only in Berlin can you hear thumping bass beneath a laundry line.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they recognize the urgency in ducking under the BART platform awning before the next delayed train hits.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Last week, a guy serenaded us on the 21 bus with acoustic covers while the Muni just sat there, stuck behind a dog on the tracks. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think we all do the 'morning café' thing. In Gràcia, we’re rushing to grab a croissant and a café amb llet for our 9-to-5.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they’re new when they enthusiastically follow all the tourist maps but still ask locals for directions to La Rambla.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look surprised when the Tube's delayed. Locals just shrug and head for the nearest coffee shop like it's a normal Tuesday.
Last night on the 196, heard a guy singing opera to the driver. He had the whole bus chuckling by the end. Only in Brixton!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I found myself in line behind an old man arguing with the bus driver about his missing discount card. Only in Neukölln, right?

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find a club that opens for breakfast after a night of partying. It's wild to see folks dancing at 8 AM while eating currywurst.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their reaction to BART delays—while newbies panic, we just grab a burrito and stroll to the nearest bus stop.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 5 this morning, driver stopped for a full minute to coax a pigeon off the tracks. Pigeons really know how to hold up a line!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists rave about our 'hidden' squares, but miss that locals avoid them during siesta. They’re quiet for a reason after lunch.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they order coffee—still asking for 'un café con leche' instead of 'un cortado' like a true local.
